title: lycopus americanus subsp. laurentianus
intro: Lycopus americanus subsp. laurentianus, also known as American water horehound, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to eastern North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits of American water horehound include its ability to treat digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to reduce stress and anxiety. American water horehound can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal remedies to soothe coughs, calm digestive issues, and promote overall well-being.
